elements.sent-2411-001: RIS, as an efficient tool to improve receive signal-to-noise ratio, extend coverage and create more spatial diversity, is viewed as a most promising technique for the future wireless networks like 6G.
elements.sent-2411-002: As you know, IRS is very suitable for a special wireless scenario with wireless link between BS and users being completely blocked.
elements.sent-2411-003: In this paper, we extend its applications to a general scenario, i.e., rank-deficient-channel, particularly some extremely low-rank ones such as no link, and line-of-sight (LoS).
elements.sent-2411-004: Actually, there are several potential important low-rank applications of like satellite, UAV communications, marine, and deep-space communications.
elements.sent-2411-005: In such a situation, it is found that RIS may make a dramatic DoF enhancement over no RIS.
elements.sent-2411-006: By using a distributed RIS placement, the DoF of channels from BS to users may be even boosted from a low-rank like 0/1 to full-rank.
elements.sent-2411-007: This will achieve an extremely rate improvement via multiple spatial streams transmission per user.
elements.sent-2411-008: In this paper, we present a complete review of make a in-depth discussion on DoF effect of RIS.
elements.section-2411-001: RIS-aided Wireless Network: concept and system
elements.sent-2411-009: Reference~\cite{Shu2017Secure proposed a secure and precise transmission scheme by combining random subcarrier selection and directional modulation (DM), which enhances system security by concentrating signal power in the small neighbouring region around the legitimate user.}
elements.sent-2411-010: Low-cost RISs can be deployed in wireless environments to provide cascaded links, a key technology for improving system performance.
elements.sent-2411-011: In~\cite{Shu2021 , a RIS-assisted DM network was proposed to transmit two confidential data streams, achieving twice the secrecy rate of single-stream transmission in the high signal-to-noise ratio region.}
elements.sent-2411-012: A distributed RIS-assisted transmission rate enhancement method was studied in~\cite{Yang2024 .}
elements.sent-2411-013: By dividing a large RIS into multiple small RIS for multi-stream transmission, the transmission rate was improved compared to a large RIS-assisted transmission.
elements.sent-2411-014: Fig.~\ref{Fig-SysMod shows a typical three-node wireless network aided by RIS.}

elements.sent-2411-017: It is assumed that $M$ and $N$ are far larger than $K$ with $N\ge M$ due to a small size of mobile terminal at user.

elements.sent-2411-020: In a rich-scattered city environment, three channel matrices may reach up to full-rank.
elements.sent-2411-021: However, in this paper, we will focus on a very typical kind of channels, called rank-deficient channel.
elements.sent-2411-022: Such a channel has the following property: rank$(\mathbf{H )<K$.}
elements.sent-2411-023: It is very evident that such a channel needs RIS to improve from its channel rank less than $K$ to $K$, full-rank.
elements.sent-2411-024: In particular, when rank$(\mathbf{H )=0$ or 1, i.e., an extremely low-rank scenario, the enhanced-DoF role of RIS will become a dominant factor to boost the system rank in order to achieve a high-rate transmission.}
elements.sent-2411-025: The former rank$(\mathbf{H )=0$, i.e. $\mathbf{H =\mathbf{0}$, means that the channel from BS to user is blocked fully with no signal energy received by user.}}
elements.sent-2411-026: The latter rank$(\mathbf{H )=1$ implies that the channel is line-of-sight (LoS) channel or additive white Gaussian noise (AWGN) channel.}
elements.sent-2411-027: Even when BS and user are employed with multiple antennas, there is only a single message stream transmitted from BS to user in LoS channel.
elements.sent-2411-028: Clearly, the above two channels demand RIS to improve their channel ranks to implement a multiple-stream transmission from BS to user.
elements.sent-2411-029: It is particularly noted that several potential applications like deep-space, marine, UAV and satellite communications are typically rank-deficient or even low-rank.
elements.sent-2411-030: In the next two sections, we will discuss how to make such a significant enhancement in rank or DoF with the help of RIS.
elements.section-2411-002: DoF Improvement and Beamforming in Typical Rank-deficient Channels
elements.sent-2411-031: In this paper, we divide wireless channels with MIMO into two typical channels: rank-deficient and full-rank.
elements.sent-2411-032: In a rich-scatted environment, the channel from BS to user as shown in Fig.~\ref{Fig-1 is viewed a full-rank channel.}
elements.sent-2411-033: The conventional LoS channel without reflecting and scattered paths is a rank-deficient channel with rank-one as shown in Fig. \ref{fig:1(c) , which is an extremely low-rank channel.}
elements.sent-2411-034: However, the lowest-rank channel as shown in Fig. \ref{fig:1(e) is a rank-zero channel which is completely blocked such that the receiver at user cannot receive any power from the signal transmitted by BS, called no-link channel in what follows.}
elements.sent-2411-035: In Fig.~\ref{fig:1(d) , a far-field RIS-aided wireless network in LoS channel is plotted.}
elements.sent-2411-036: Clearly, the channel increases from one (no RIS) in Fig. \ref{fig:1(c) to two (RIS-aided) in Fig.~\ref{fig:1(d) where both channels from BS to RIS and {RIS to user are assumed to be LoS channels.}}}
elements.sent-2411-037: Furthermore, if both reflected channels are full-rank, the channel rank is boosted up to $\min(M, N, K)=K$ considering $M$ and $N$ is far larger than $K$.
elements.sent-2411-038: Similarly, as shown in Figs.~\ref{fig:1(b) and \ref{fig:1(f) , in a no-link channel, due to the introduction of RIS, the channel rank may be enhanced from 0 in Fig. \ref{fig:1(e) to the range from 1 to $K$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-039: In summary, for a rank-deficient channel with rank $k$ more than LoS, its rank is also improved from $k$ to the range from $k+1$ to full-rank ($K$).
elements.sent-2411-040: Additionally, it is particularly pointed out that there are no DoF gain achieved in a scenario where all channels in Fig. 1 are full-rank, However, reflecting array gain and diversity gain created by RIS always exist.

elements.section-2411-003: DoF Boosting and Beamforming via distributed IRSs in LoS Channels
elements.figure-2411-003: \includegraphics[width=0.40\textwidth, trim = 20 20 20 20,clip]{fig_3.eps} \caption{Diagram of distributed multi-RIS-aided Wireless Network.}\label{Fig-3}
elements.sent-2411-041: In this section, we will discuss a special scenario both channels from BS to RIS and RIS to user as shown in Fig.~\ref{Fig-3 , are LoS channels.}
elements.sent-2411-042: Is there any way to further boost the value of system DoF.
elements.sent-2411-043: In the following, a distributed multi-RIS concept is proposed to make a dramatic DoF enhancement.
elements.sent-2411-044: In accordance with basic principle of MIMO system, DoF is vital to boost rate performance.
elements.sent-2411-045: In this novel distributed model, $J$ RISs are distributed deployed to create more DoFs.
elements.sent-2411-046: Subsequently, a point-to-point $J$-stream transmission can be achieved in this rank-deficient-channel scenario.
elements.sent-2411-047: Moreover, a significant rate enhancement can be achieved by this model.
elements.sent-2411-048: Due to the above major advantages of RIS and its ability to make a striking DoF improvement in low-rank channels, RIS may extend its application to several new fields such as near-sea, near-space, and UAV communications.
elements.sent-2411-049: However, there still are several open challenging problems to address in RIS fields.
elements.sent-2411-050: Here, we list several important ones of them as follows:.
elements.sent-2411-051: Similar to a movable antenna array, a movable RIS may be introduced to further optimize the space position of RIS over a given 3D area to harvest the performance gains.
elements.sent-2411-052: Additionally, how about its performance upper bounds?.
elements.sent-2411-053: In near field, how to compute and evaluate the DoF value of RIS when the distance from user to RIS is less than the Rayleigh distance? If it is hard, its DoF upper or lower bounds may be derived.
elements.sent-2411-054: Moreover, the corresponding beamforming and phase adjusting methods is also designed to implement a multi-stream transmission from BS to user.
elements.sent-2411-055: In this article, the great potential of RIS has been highlighted as a crucial DoF enhancement tool for several typical low-rank scenarios such as marine, UAV, satellite and deep-space communications.
elements.sent-2411-056: A distributed multi-RIS deployment was proposed to achieve a significant rank enhancement for low-rank wireless situations.
elements.sent-2411-057: There are still several challenging problems ahead to exploit its great potential of the technology.
elements.sent-2411-058: Also, we have raised several new open important research problems.
elements.sent-2411-059: Finally, in our view, a distributed multi-RIS will achieve wide diverse promising applications in the coming future.. \renewcommand\refname{References \bibliographystyle{IEEEtran.}}
elements.sent-2411-060: With the development of sixth-generation (6G) communication, there are higher requirements for system capacity, security, and reliability.
elements.sent-2411-061: In~\cite{Liu2023 , near-field communication (NFC) was discussed as an essential technology for 6G networks, driven by the adoption of extremely large-scale antenna arrays and high frequencies.}
elements.sent-2411-062: NFC leverages near-field spherical-wave propagation to enhance beamfocusing and improve performance metrics such as signal-to-noise ratio and channel capacity.
elements.sent-2411-063: Similarly, reference~\cite{Wu2019 studied a joint active and passive beamforming design in RIS-aided systems, showing that RIS can achieve comparable performance to massive MIMO systems but with reduced hardware complexity and energy consumption.}
elements.sent-2411-064: Furthermore, reference~\cite{Pan2020 analyzed RIS deployment at cell boundaries to mitigate inter-cell interference, highlighting its role in enhancing spectral efficiency through spatial multiplexing in multicell MIMO systems.}
elements.sent-2411-065: RIS can be combined with relay stations to improve the coverage and transmission rate of the wireless networks.
elements.sent-2411-066: In~\cite{Wang2022 , a dual-slot system model with simultaneous deployment of relay stations and RIS was studied, and compared with the single antenna relay network, the performance gain of 85\% was achieved in high signal-to-noise ratio areas.}
elements.sent-2411-067: As studied above, RIS provides degrees of freedom (DoF) and improves performance compared to the systems without RIS.
elements.sent-2411-068: A joint small-scale spatiotemporal correlation model was proposed and analyzed for DoF under isotropic scattering in~\cite{Sun2021 .}
elements.sent-2411-069: Active RIS can improve the sum-of-degrees-of-freedom (sum-DoF) of dual-user MIMO systems with an equal number of antennas at both the transmitting and receiving ends.
elements.sent-2411-070: In~\cite{Zheng2023, Liu2024 , the sum-DoF was further studied for any number of receiving and transmitting antennas, and the existence of DoF gain is related to the rate and the number of transmitting and receiving antennas.}
elements.sent-2411-071: Based on information theory, the DoF of the joint and independent transmission of information for RIS and transmitter was investigated in\cite{Cheng2024 .}
elements.sent-2411-072: The channel rank of the non-zero direct path can eliminate the phase ambiguity between the RF signal and RIS phase shift caused by multiplicative fading.
elements.sent-2411-073: For the rank-deficient channel, when the rank of the channel from the transmitter to the receiver is large and/or the rank of the channel from the transmitter to the RIS is small, the DoF can be significantly improved~\cite{Chae2023 .}
elements.sent-2411-074: Reference~\cite{Wang2024 studied the reconfigurable distributed antenna and reflecting surface (RDARS) assisted MIMO system and obtained additional DoF through mode selection of each reflection element.}
elements.sent-2411-075: To fully exploit the DoF of RIS, a multi-layer refractive RIS-assisted receiver was proposed~\cite{An2024 , and a robust optimization framework of a low-complexity synchronous wireless information and power transmission scheme was designed to enhance receiver performance.}
elements.sent-2411-076: In~\cite{Zhang2024 , the DoF of the RIS-assisted network was improved through a deep-learning approach.}
elements.sent-2411-077: Fig.~\ref{Fig-1 shows a typical three-node wireless network aided by RIS.}

elements.sent-2411-084: Reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS), as an efficient tool to improve receive signal-to-noise ratio, extend coverage and create more spatial diversity, is viewed as a most promising technique for the future wireless networks like 6G.
elements.sent-2411-085: As you know, RIS is very suitable for a special wireless scenario with wireless link between BS and users being completely blocked, i.e., no link.
elements.sent-2411-086: In this paper, we extend its applications to a general scenario, i.e., rank-deficient channel, particularly some extremely low-rank ones such as no link, and line-of-sight (LoS, rank-one).
elements.sent-2411-087: Actually, there are several potential important low-rank applications like low-altitude, satellite, UAV, marine, and deep-space communications.
elements.sent-2411-088: In such a situation, it is found that RIS may make a dramatic degrees of freedom (DoF) enhancement over no RIS.
elements.sent-2411-089: By using a distributed RISs placement, the DoF of channel from BS to user in LoS channel may be even boosted from a low-rank like 0/1 to full-rank.
elements.sent-2411-090: This will achieve an extremely rate improvement via spatial parallel multiple-stream transmission from BS to user.
elements.sent-2411-091: In this paper, we present a complete review of making an in-depth discussion on DoF effect of RIS.
elements.sent-2411-092: In~\cite{Shu2021 , a RIS-assisted DM network was proposed to transmit two confidential data streams from Alice to Bob, achieving twice the secrecy rate of single-stream transmission in the high signal-to-noise ratio region.}
elements.sent-2411-093: It is particularly noted that the conventional DM is very suitable for line-of-sight (LoS) channel with rank-one, and only transmits only single bit stream from base station (BS) to user.
elements.sent-2411-094: In~\cite{Yang2024 , a distributed RIS-assisted transmission rate enhancement method in LoS channel was studied to increase channel rank from one to $K$.}
elements.sent-2411-095: NFC leverages near-field spherical-wave propagation to enhance beam focusing and improve performance metrics such as signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and channel capacity.
elements.sent-2411-096: In~\cite{Wang2022 , RIS can be combined with relay stations to improve the coverage and transmission rate of the wireless networks.}
elements.sent-2411-097: Here, a dual-slot system model with simultaneous deployment of relay stations and RIS was studied, and compared with the single antenna relay network, the performance gain of 85\% was achieved in the high SNR region.
elements.sent-2411-098: Active RIS can improve the sum of DoFs (sum-DoF) of dual-user MIMO systems with an equal number of antennas at both the transmitting and receiving ends.
elements.sent-2411-099: Reference~\cite{Wang2024 studied the reconfigurable distributed antenna and reflecting surface assisted MIMO system and obtained additional DoF through mode selection of each reflection element.}

elements.section-2411-007: DoF Boosting and Beamforming via distributed RISs in LoS Channels
elements.sent-2411-133: In this section, we will discuss a special scenario with both channels from BS to RIS and RIS to user as shown in Fig.~\ref{Fig-3 being LoS channels.}
elements.sent-2411-134: Is there any way to further boost the DoF value of system? In the following, a distributed multi-RIS concept is proposed to make a dramatic DoF enhancement~\cite{Yang2024 in all-LoS scenario.}
elements.sent-2411-135: However, it is particularly noted that the idea is also extended to the cases of all-reflected channels with channel rank being larger than or equal to one.
elements.sent-2411-136: In general, a traditional low-rank network has one or two DoFs, which will seriously limit its rate performance.
elements.sent-2411-137: In this section, a novel distributed system model with $J$ RISs as shown in Fig. \ref{Fig-3 are distributed deployed to create more DoFs.}
elements.sent-2411-138: The corresponding channel matrices from BS to RIS $j$ and RIS $j$ to user are denoted as $\mathbf{G _{BR ^j$ and $\mathbf{G _{RU ^j$, respectively.}}}}

elements.sent-2411-153: In this article, the great potential of RIS has been highlighted as a crucial DoF enhancement tool for several typical low-rank future application scenarios such as marine, low-altitude, UAV, satellite and deep-space communications.
elements.sent-2411-154: Finally, in our view, a distributed multi-RIS will achieve wide diverse promising applications in the coming future.

elements.figure-2411-015: \subfloat[]{\label{fig:7a} \includegraphics[width=3in, trim = 20 1 1 10,clip]{fig_7a.eps}}\hfil \subfloat[]{\label{fig:7b} \includegraphics[width=3in, trim = 20 1 1 10,clip]{fig_7b.eps}} \caption{(a) The achievable rate versus noise power $\sigma_r^2$ at RIS in scenarios with distributed RISs; (b) The achievable rate versus noise power $\sigma_u^2$ at user in scenarios with distributed RISs.}\label{Fig-7}
elements.sent-2411-181: To evaluate the rate performance enhancement achieved by DoF increasement due to the introduction of distributed RISs, we will present numerical simulations as follows.
elements.sent-2411-182: System parameters are set as follows: the distances from BS to RIS, RIS to user, and BS to user are 82 m, 28 m, and 100 m, respectively; the number of antennas at BS, the number of RIS elements, and the number of antennas at user are $M = 128$, $N = 600$, and $K = 4$, respectively.
elements.sent-2411-183: Fig. \ref{fig:7a illustrates the curves of achievable rate versus noise power $\sigma_r^2$ at RIS obtained by distributed RISs with $\sigma_u^2 = -70$ dBm, where the transmitting beamforming vector, phase shifting matrix, and receive beamforming vector are designed by null-space-projection, phase alignment, and zero-forcing, respectively.}
elements.sent-2411-184: Observing this figure, we find: as noise power $\sigma_r^2$ at RIS increases from -120 dBm to -60 dBm, the achieved rate gains over no RIS gradually decrease and converge to zero.
elements.sent-2411-185: When the number of distributed RISs is greater than or equal to 2, i.e., the system DoF increase by 2 or more, the performance is obviously improved compared with no RIS under high noise power, i.e., $\sigma_r^2=-60$ dBm.
elements.sent-2411-186: As the number of distributed RISs grows from one to four, the achieved rate gains are as follows: about 0.7, 1.9, 2.7, and 3.5 times over no RIS under the same power-sum constraint and $\sigma_r^2=-90$ dBm.
elements.sent-2411-187: According to this trend, it can be inferred that increasing the number of distributed RISs and reducing noise power $\sigma_r^2$ at RIS will further improve the rate performance.
elements.sent-2411-188: Fig. \ref{fig:7b illustrates the curves of achievable rate versus noise power $\sigma_u^2$ at user obtained by distributed RISs with $\sigma_r^2 = -90$ dBm.}
elements.sent-2411-189: From this figure, it is seen: as noise power $\sigma_u^2$ at user varies from -120 dBm to -60 dBm, the achieved rate gains over no RIS gradually decrease.
elements.sent-2411-190: In summary, we have the following conclusions: (1) Increasing the number of distributed RISs may boost the value of system rank or DoF such that a significant rate gain can be attained, (2) low-noise RIS and receiver are also crucial for an active RIS-aided wireless network to achieve a high rate gain.

elements.sent-2411-196: By dividing a large RIS into multiple small RISs for multi-stream transmission, the transmission rate was improved compared to a large RIS-assisted transmission.
elements.sent-2411-197: In~\cite{Liu2023 , near-field communication (NFC) was discussed as an essential technology for 6G networks, which leveraged near-field spherical-wave propagation to enhance beam focusing and improve performance metrics such as SNR and channel capacity.}
elements.sent-2411-198: Similarly, the authors in~\cite{Wu2019 studied a joint active and passive beamforming design in RIS-aided systems, which showed that RIS can achieve better performance to massive MIMO systems but with reduced hardware complexity and energy consumption.}
elements.sent-2411-199: Furthermore, RIS deployment at cell boundaries was analyzed to mitigate inter-cell interference in~\cite{Pan2020 , which highlighted its role in enhancing spectral efficiency through spatial multiplexing in multicell MIMO systems.}
elements.sent-2411-200: In~\cite{Wang2022 , RIS could be combined with relay stations to improve the coverage and transmission rate of the wireless networks.}
elements.sent-2411-201: Here, a dual-slot system model with simultaneous deployment of relay stations and RIS was studied, and compared with the single-antenna relay network, a 85\% performance gain was achieved in the high SNR region.
elements.sent-2411-202: As studied above, RIS can improve the performance of wireless network, which is the result of the degrees of freedom (DoF) increased by introducing RIS.
